title: AML/CTF Fundamentals
description: Foundational module covering money laundering stages (placement, layering, integration), terrorist financing typologies, the UK regulatory framework, and the role of the FIU. Mandatory baseline for all financial crime staff.

title: KYC Onboarding Process
description: How to collect, verify and record customer identification documents during onboarding. Covers proof of identity, proof of address, beneficial owner identification, and documentation standards.

title: AML/KYC Awareness for Customer-Facing Staff
description: Tailored AML literacy for customer advisors. Focuses on recognising red flags in customer interactions without compromising the customer experience.

title: GDPR & Customer Data Handling
description: Lawful basis for processing customer personal data in AML and customer service contexts. Data minimisation, retention, and customer rights under GDPR.

title: GDPR in Financial Crime Investigations
description: How GDPR interacts with AML investigations. Lawful basis for processing sensitive data, retention requirements for SAR-related material, and the tipping-off exception.

title: Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) & Case Handling
description: Deep dive on EDD triggers, evidence-gathering for source of wealth and source of funds, structuring EDD case files, and writing audit-ready risk rationales.

title: Source of Wealth and Source of Funds Analysis
description: Distinguishing source of wealth from source of funds. Plausibility assessment, corroboration techniques, and red flag patterns in unusual or inconsistent narratives.

title: Beneficial Ownership Structures
description: Identifying ultimate beneficial owners through complex corporate structures, nominee arrangements, and trust vehicles. Use of corporate registries and adverse media.

title: PEP Screening & Management
description: Definition of PEPs, family members and close associates. Use of screening tools (World-Check, Dow Jones), senior management approval workflows, and ongoing PEP monitoring.

title: Sanctions Screening Fundamentals
description: Sanctions regimes (OFAC, EU, UK OFSI, UN), screening logic and fuzzy matching, false positive disposition, and the consequences of breach.

title: Sanctions Awareness for Customer-Facing Staff
description: Light-touch sanctions awareness for advisors. What sanctions are, how to spot a potential match in conversation, and the strict no-tip-off rule.

title: Transaction Monitoring Systems & Alert Workflows
description: How automated TM systems generate alerts (rules, thresholds, scenarios), the alert queue workflow, and case management discipline.

title: Money Laundering Typologies
description: Common ML typologies (smurfing, structuring, trade-based laundering, shell companies, layering through correspondent banking), with sector-specific case studies.

title: SAR Submission Process
description: Thresholds for SAR submission, drafting SAR narratives that meet UKFIU expectations, the role of the nominated officer, and post-submission obligations.

title: Tipping Off — Awareness & Avoidance
description: Statutory tipping-off offence under POCA. How to handle customer questions about delays or refusals without disclosing investigations.

title: Case Documentation Standards
description: Writing investigation notes and risk rationales that survive regulatory scrutiny. The 'reasonable reader' standard, audit trail expectations, and common documentation failures.

title: High-Risk Typologies Workshop
description: Advanced typology workshop covering trade-based ML, professional enablers, real-estate laundering, and crypto layering. Case-led discussion format.

title: Peer Review Exercise
description: Structured peer-review session where analysts review each other's recent cases and provide constructive challenge on rationale quality.

title: Risk-Based Thinking Workshop
description: Moving from checklist compliance to genuine risk-based judgement. How to weight risk factors, document reasoning, and apply standards consistently under pressure.

title: Fraud & AML Overlap
description: Where fraud and money laundering investigations intersect, common shared typologies, and the practical implications for case routing.

title: Consumer Duty Awareness
description: FCA Consumer Duty principles, the four outcomes, and how Consumer Duty obligations interact with AML and complaint handling.

title: Complaint Handling (FCA DISP Rules)
description: FCA DISP rules for complaint handling, statutory timeframes, escalation to FOS, and root-cause analysis of complaint themes.

title: Third-Party & Delivery Partner Risk
description: Managing AML/KYC risk on delivery partners and suppliers. Initial due diligence, periodic reviews, and exit triggers.

title: Board-Level F&FC Reporting
description: Structuring the annual MLRO report and quarterly Board updates. KRI/KPI design, narrative quality, and what regulators look for in MLRO MI.

title: Regulatory Update Briefing
description: Quarterly briefing on changes to UK and EU AML/CFT regulation, FATF guidance, and enforcement actions relevant to the sector.

title: Annual AML Mandatory Refresher
description: Annual baseline refresher required for all in-scope staff. Covers key obligations, recent enforcement, and internal policy updates.

title: Full Competency Assessment
description: End-of-year structured competency assessment combining scenario-based testing, case-file QA review, and 1:1 with line manager.

title: Difficult Conversations Workshop
description: Handling sensitive customer interactions involving suspected vulnerability, complaints, or potential financial abuse — while respecting tipping-off rules.

title: ICA Certificate in AML — Enrolment
description: Enrolment in the International Compliance Association Certificate in Anti-Money Laundering — externally accredited foundation qualification.
